name: Bobby Short
abstract: Robert Waltrip Short (September 15, 1924 – March 21, 2005) was an American cabaret singer and pianist who interpreted songs by popular composers from the first half of the 20th century such as Rodgers and Hart, Cole Porter, Jerome Kern, Harold Arlen, Richard A. Whiting, Vernon Duke, Noël Coward and George and Ira Gershwin.

about_quotes.speaker: Jean Bach
about_quotes.evidence: Bobby Short, who is my oldest friend and I, we were celebrating having known each other for 40 years
about_quotes.doc_title: Jean Bach interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Bobby Short always laughs when he hears that... Bobby [Short, the pianist and singer at the Café Carlisle] was very friendly with me... I’m always grateful to him, because he really set me up in that room
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Anne Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: I played actually opposite Bobby Short in his first gig at the — it wasn’t the Carlyle then it was the Beverly Hotel, and I was the other, the off piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Anne Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: I’m at the Carlyle and he added three saxes, two trumpets and a trombone about five years ago. So I do that, among other things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: in 1997 I became Bobby Short’s musical director... I wound up doing a thousand one- hour shows with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: And we accompanied him with the band you know, came in on his finales and so forth.
